incorporations.original: AND here we come to our chief
incorporations.replacement: AND so we come to our chief
incorporations.reason: "so" better renders the Italian "eccoci" (here we are / and so), signalling a rhetorical transition rather than spatial arrival

incorporations.original: by the mysterious reactions of his spirit
incorporations.replacement: through the mysterious reactions of his soul
incorporations.reason: "soul" for "animo" is more natural in English literary register; "through" better conveys the causal sense of "per"

incorporations.original: despite his pretension to being a positive man
incorporations.replacement: despite his pretension to being a practical man
incorporations.reason: Italian "uomo positivo" in this context means a man of practical, empirical temper, not a man who is "positive" in the modern English sense; Gemini's "practical" avoids the ambiguity

incorporations.original: With an irony that seemed cutting
incorporations.replacement: With an irony that seemed cruel
incorporations.reason: "sanguinosa" literally means blood-drawing / cruel; "cutting" is weaker and loses the visceral quality

incorporations.original: through the streets of the capital
incorporations.replacement: through the streets of the metropolis
incorporations.reason: "metropoli" is a direct cognate; "capital" flattens the grandiose connotation that Crespi intends — Orsini parading through a great world-city

incorporations.original: so as to wrest from him a full confession
incorporations.replacement: in order to wring from him a full confession
incorporations.reason: "wring" better conveys the forcible extraction implied by "strappargli" (to tear/wrench out)

incorporations.original: standing before a snarling mongrel without trembling
incorporations.replacement: standing without trembling before a snarling cur
incorporations.reason: "cur" is more period-appropriate and dismissive than "mongrel" for the contemptuous Italian "botolo ringhioso" (a snarling little mutt)

incorporations.original: Jules Favre was not the intellect to spoil
incorporations.replacement: Giulio Favre was not the manner of intellect to ruin
incorporations.reason: Retains the Italian form "Giulio" consistent with the source and the rest of the translation; "ruin" and the expanded phrasing better convey "guastare" (to spoil/wreck) with appropriate gravity

incorporations.original: He brought to it the powerful hand of the artist
incorporations.replacement: He laid upon it the powerful hand of the artist
incorporations.reason: "laid upon it the hand" mirrors the Italian "vi pose la mano" (placed his hand upon it), capturing the deliberate, artisanal gesture Crespi describes

edgren_influences.note: Claude's draft rendered "ingegno destro" as "a talent adroit"; Gemini used "a mind adept". Edgren explicitly lists "genius" as a primary sense of ingegno. The final uses "a genius adroit", selecting the Edgren sense that best matches the elevated register and the specific context of conspiratorial brilliance.

Both drafts use "maladies" or "illnesses". The final keeps "maladies" (matching Claude), which aligns with Edgren's listed equivalent and preserves the clinical-yet-literary tone Crespi employs when diagnosing Orsini's temperamental deficiencies. "Illnesses" (Gemini) would have been too plain.

Edgren confirms "sum" as the primary sense of "somma", supporting the retention of the mathematical metaphor ("the sum of trifles patiently worked with a chisel") rather than paraphrasing to "total" or "aggregate".
